name: GPT-5.5 (xHigh)
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; 'xhigh' (maximum) reasoning-effort configuration
note: Highest reasoning-effort sibling of the base 'GPT-5.5' entry ('reserved for the hardest quality-first workloads' per OpenAI's own guidance). Same weights/pricing/context as the default and High siblings; distinguished by reasoning.effort=xhigh.

results.notes: Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier1-3 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.
Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier4 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Codex harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 26.6% / Score 47.9% (rank 10). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('xHigh'). Score component used per corpus convention.

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 component), independently measured on the dedicated 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (URL slug and page title both confirm this is the xhigh-effort config, distinct from the 'GPT-5.5 (high)' page which scores differently on the same eval). Read by exact rank-position matching against AA's live ranked benchmark chart (GPT-5.5 (xhigh) placed between Claude Opus 4.7 (max) and GPT-5.5 (high), both at 50%). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry. [score corrected during round-6 completeness verify: proposed 50 -> 49.7] [score corrected during round-12 accuracy re-verify: 49.7 -> 50, per verifier: Loaded artificialanalysis.ai/models/gpt-5-5 live in browser. In the GDPval-AA v2 ranked bar chart (29 models), 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' sits at rank 16 with a score of 50% (an integer, tied with Claude Opus 4.7 (max) directly above it at 50%; GPT-5.5 (high)] [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (50%) to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale (matching the majority convention used by e.g. Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, Claude Fable 5=1932). Converted via Artificial Analysis's own documented formula, normalized=(Elo-500)/2000, i.e. raw=50*20+500=1500. This formula is independently cross-validated against Kimi K2.7 Code's directly-disclosed embedded-JSON raw Elo (1186.79 for a displayed 34%, matching the formula almost exactly).]

Terminal-Bench v2.1 (AA's harness), independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page. Distinct data point from OpenAI's own self-reported 'Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.7%' figure (different harness version/measurement); this catalog entry currently has no terminal-bench-2 result at all, so this is new, not a duplicate.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (AA's own methodology, likely differs from OpenAI's no-tools condition -- OpenAI's own blog cites 41.4%/52.2% depending on source, none of which is on this catalog entry). Not previously tracked here.

GPQA Diamond, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page -- ranked #1-2 of 28 models tracked (94%, tied with GPT-5.6 Sol (max)). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry. [score corrected during round-12 accuracy re-verify: 94 -> 93.5, per verifier: Fetched artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/gpqa-diamond, which explicitly states in prose: 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh) with a score of 93.5%'. The underlying chart JSON confirms GPQA Diamond=0.935353535353535 for label 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' (detailsUrl /models/gpt-5]
Table also glimpsed once before (2026-07-17 pass) but not acted on since swe-bench was out of that round's scope; confirmed and added this round.

SealedBench sealed capability index — composite of capability, token efficiency, speed and cost; rank 9/28 on the 2026-07-22 run (sealedbench.com). Listed as "GPT-5.5" (OpenAI). Evaluated at the highest available thinking setting. Attached to the xHigh effort sibling of GPT-5.5 (SealedBench uses each model's highest available thinking setting).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed as a distinct, independently-benchmarked configuration on Artificial Analysis (model page 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', explicitly labeled 'Reasoning Effort Label: xhigh' on direct fetch; Intelligence Index score 55, rank #7/188, strictly ahead of the 'high' sibling's 53/#12). benchlm.ai's benchmark table for GPT-5.5 explicitly attributes its FrontierMath v2 figures to 'xhigh effort' (Tier 1-3: 51.7%, Tier 4: 35.4%) — these are numerically identical to the FrontierMath scores already stored on the pre-existing base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry, strongly suggesting that entry's headline benchmark numbers are actually xhigh-tier results mislabeled as the plain/default config. Flagging for the ingesting pipeline to consider migrating those two FrontierMath results (and possibly GDPval/BrowseComp/SWE-Bench Pro/Terminal-Bench, which are likely also xhigh headline figures) from the base entry onto this xHigh entry, and to set the base entry's isBestConfig to false since this sibling is now the true best-of-cohort.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark completeness check (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu): all seven resolve to omitted_no_evidence -- no citable xhigh-specific (or even effort-unspecified) score exists for any of them. (1) Official materials: three independent close-readings of openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5 (kingy.ai's '9 numbers quoted verbatim from OpenAI's evaluation tables', vellum.ai's 16-benchmark comprehensive listing, and alexlavaee.me's review) all enumerate OpenAI's launch benchmarks in full and none lists MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, or MMMU -- OpenAI's own launch page evidently never reported these seven. (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis maintains dedicated AIME-2025, LiveCodeBench, and MATH-500 leaderboards but each is capped at a static 7-of-269/343/201 'spotlight' model set that excludes every GPT-5.5 variant entirely -- AA has not run these three evals on GPT-5.5 at all. AA's own 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' model page (fetched live) lists its full tracked-eval set (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, MMMU-Pro) and it contains no MMLU, base MMMU, HumanEval, or SWE-bench (classic/Verified) entry -- AA has fully retired these in favor of harder successors (MMLU-Pro, MMMU-Pro, SWE-bench Pro, etc.) for frontier-tier models by mid-2026. (3) Sibling cross-reference: neither the base 'GPT-5.5' nor 'GPT-5.5 (High)' corpus entries carry any of these seven benchmarks either, a consistent absence across the whole family already vetted in prior rounds. One contested exception was investigated and rejected: TokenMix.ai and marc0.dev both circulate an unsourced 'SWE-Bench Verified 88.7% / MMLU 92.4%' pair attributed loosely to 'OpenAI announcement', but this pair appears nowhere in the three careful launch-post transcriptions above, is not effort-specific, and could not be corroborated on OpenAI's own page (blocked from direct fetch) or on Vals.ai/epoch.ai (access blocked/no data returned) -- treated as unreliable and not added. This matches the taxonomy's own framing of mmlu ('largely saturated at the frontier') and humaneval ('long-saturated at the frontier') as benchmarks frontier labs are expected to stop publishing.

2026-07-17 second-pass deep dive on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, using source types NOT touched by the 2026-07-16 pass (official launch page, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM). All four gaps still resolve to omitted_no_evidence after checking all 6 prescribed new source types:

(1) Stanford HELM: Direct fetch of crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/groups/core_scenarios.json returned HTTP 404 (path/schema has moved or requires JS rendering); web search against crfm.stanford.edu confirms HELM's evaluated-model rosters (Capabilities, Lite) list GPT-4o/Claude/Gemini-era models but no GPT-5.5 of any effort variant -- HELM has not run this model at all, so no independent MMLU (or any) score exists there to retrieve.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Targeted arXiv searches ("GPT-5.5 MMLU", AIME/MATH/HumanEval combinations) surface only GPT-5/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2 papers -- no arXiv paper was found reporting a GPT-5.5 baseline number on any of the four benchmarks. The one paper that does cite "GPT-5.5" in its bibliography (arXiv 2605.00674, "Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena...") was fetched directly and confirmed to have no GPT-5.5 row in its actual results tables, only a bibliographic citation. (semanticscholar.org direct fetch was blocked by network/enterprise policy, so coverage here is arXiv-only, supplemented by web search.)

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: Searched EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness and groq/openbench (a 95+-benchmark harness explicitly covering MMLU, GPQA, HumanEval, and competition math incl. AIME) for any committed run artifacts against GPT-5.5. Found no results.json, leaderboard entry, or PR referencing GPT-5.5 in either repo.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's gpt-5.5 and gpt-5.5-pro pages exist but render benchmark data client-side (not present in fetched content) and OpenRouter is known to source its numbers from Artificial Analysis, already exhausted in round 1. Scale AI SEAL: web search confirms GPT-5.5 has "no standardized public-set entry" on SEAL as of late June 2026 -- only a vendor-reported aggregate figure circulates (the same unsourced 88.7%/92.4% SWE-bench/MMLU pair already investigated and rejected in the prior round). LiveBench.ai and PapersWithCode returned no retrievable GPT-5.5 data (JS-rendered / redirect). AskClash.ai's dedicated 'gpt-5-5-xhigh' leaderboard page is JS-rendered and returned no populated benchmark values on fetch. LM Council (lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks) was the most substantive new find: it has explicit per-model tables for GPQA Diamond, SWE-bench Verified, FrontierMath Tiers 1-3/Tier 4, WeirdML v2, GSO, and OTIS Mock AIME 2024-25 for 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', but confirmed NO table for MMLU, MMLU-Pro, HumanEval, or HumanEval+ anywhere on the page, and its one MATH-adjacent table ('MATH Level 5') does not include GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at all. Its one AIME-adjacent entry, 'OTIS Mock AIME 2024-25' (GPT-5.5 (xhigh): 100.0% +/-0.0), was investigated and REJECTED for two independent reasons: (a) OTIS Mock AIME is a distinct benchmark from the taxonomy's 'aime' slug (\"American Invitational Mathematics Examination, pass@1\" -- the real competition), not an accepted proxy, and no otis/mock-aime slug exists in corpus/benchmarks.json to place it under instead; (b) the same page's FrontierMath figures for this exact model (Tiers 1-3: 85.3%, Tier 4: 72.5%) sharply contradict the well-corroborated benchlm.ai/Epoch AI figures already in this entry (51.7%/35.4%), a red flag strong enough to treat the entire source as unreliable/possibly synthetic rather than cite any of its numbers.

(5) Non-English-language sources: Chinese (Zhihu, chatgpt-china.org) and Japanese (oishillc.jp AI Lab OISHI complete guide, aipicks.jp) coverage was checked. None reports MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for GPT-5.5. Notably, oishillc.jp's independent Japanese-language review explicitly states (translated): \"The official System Card for GPT-5.5 includes no listings of Japanese benchmarks and major English benchmarks like MMLU lack specific reporting\" -- an independent non-English corroboration of the same absence the first pass already found on the English launch materials. Korean sources (gpters.org, wikidocs.net) likewise surfaced no MMLU/AIME-specific figures for GPT-5.5.

(6) Sibling-model comparison tables: layerlens.ai's GLM-5 benchmark review (a sibling-family comparison piece) does not include any GPT-5.5 comparison data at all. OpenRouter's GPT-5.5 Pro page (closest sibling variant) has no populated comparison table in its fetched content. No sibling-model source was found carrying a citable MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for the base or xhigh GPT-5.5 config.

Also encountered but explicitly rejected as non-citable during this pass (content-mill/SEO sites with no evaluation methodology, no source citations, or numbers already flagged unreliable in the prior round): automatio.ai (MMLU 92.5%, AIME 100%, MATH 98%, HumanEval 94.2% -- \"page lists no source citations... does not specify which reasoning effort level\"); o-mega.ai (MMLU 92.4%, the same unsourced figure already rejected in the 2026-07-16 pass); promptchief.tech (table structure present but data not retrievable, states scores are \"from official model cards and papers\" yet none of those actually report this). None of these meet the bar of a real, citable, independent methodology.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval remain omitted_no_evidence after this second, broader pass across all 6 prescribed new source types (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval-harness, other leaderboards/aggregators, non-English sources, sibling-model tables). This is consistent with the taxonomy's own framing of these four as saturated/retired frontier benchmarks that OpenAI and independent evaluators alike have stopped running or reporting for the GPT-5.5 generation.

2026-07-18 THIRD-PASS deep dive on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, using the 5 prescribed NEW source types not touched by round 1 (official launch/Epoch/Vals/LMArena/BenchLM/llm-stats) or round 2 (HELM/arXiv/GitHub-harness-logs/other-leaderboards/non-English/sibling-tables). All four gaps still resolve to omitted_no_evidence.

(1) Wayback Machine -- GENUINE ATTEMPT, INFRASTRUCTURE-BLOCKED (inconclusive, not a resolution either way): archive.org's own 'available' API confirmed a real snapshot exists (web.archive.org/web/20260423214056/https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5/, captured ~2.5h after the 2026-04-23 launch). However every attempt to actually read that snapshot's content failed: the WebFetch tool categorically refuses web.archive.org URLs ('unable to fetch'), and 6 separate direct-curl retries spread across several minutes of this session all returned HTTP 429 'Too Many Requests' from archive.org's nginx front end (including via the raw id_ modifier and different user-agents). An independent proxy (r.jina.ai) confirmed the sandbox's outbound network (ASN AS9009) is currently flagged for 'bad network reputation' and blocked/throttled across multiple services, which explains the persistent failures -- this is an environment-level access limitation, not a lack of effort, and the archived content could not be compared against the live page in this pass. CDX snapshot queries for llm-stats.com and artificialanalysis.ai model pages from just after release were attempted for the same reason and hit the same 429 wall.

(2) Conference talks/keynote transcripts: Searched for OpenAI DevDay 2026 GPT-5.5 keynote coverage and podcast interviews discussing GPT-5.5 benchmarks. No DevDay keynote or podcast transcript tied specifically to the GPT-5.5 release was found; all available coverage traces back to the blog-post-only launch (openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5). No verbal MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure surfaced from this channel.

(3) Investor/press materials: Checked Microsoft's FY2026 Q3 earnings call transcript (2026-04-29, the report closest to GPT-5.5's 2026-04-23 release) -- search results reference GPT-5 and GPT-5.2 token-throughput commentary but contain no mention of GPT-5.5 at all, let alone a benchmark figure. OpenAI itself has no public investor deck (not an SEC filer). No investor/press material citing mmlu/math/aime/humaneval for GPT-5.5 was found.

(4) GitHub issues/SDK repo discussions: Searched openai-python, openai-node, openai-cookbook, and openai/evals (issues, code, and GraphQL discussions) for 'GPT-5.5 MMLU'-type questions -- no hits in any of the official OpenAI SDK repos. Broadened to a full GitHub code search across all public repos combining gpt-5.5 with mmlu/math-500/aime/humaneval, which surfaced several independent third-party eval-harness repos actually running or cataloging GPT-5.5 -- all investigated and rejected:
  - abhijithreddy05/model-catalog's gpt-5.5-pro.txt catalog explicitly marks mmlu, gpqa, humaneval, and mmmu as 'not published' (with math/FrontierMath Tier 4 at 39.6% instead) -- an independent community corroboration of the absence, not a score.
  - witanlabs/contamination-probes ran a real TS-Guessing contamination probe against gpt-5.5 using genuine MMLU Philosophy questions (masked-distractor recall), producing exact/norm-match rates of 6.7%/24.4% -- this measures verbatim memorization/contamination, not benchmark accuracy, so it cannot substitute for an 'mmlu' pass-rate score; rejected as a different metric entirely.
  - aashwindev/openfugu published a 'gpt-5.5' MMLU score of 93.5% (600-question run) with a convincing-looking README and $847 cost ledger -- but direct inspection of its underlying task file (benchmarks/tasks/mmlu_professional.jsonl) showed the 'questions' are fabricated placeholder templates ('MMLU abstract_algebra question 1: select the best answer' with choices 'A) option 1' / 'B) option 2' / etc., not real MMLU items) -- REJECTED as synthetic/fake data dressed up as a real eval run, not a genuine measurement.
  - archit0/switchboard ran a real HumanEval subset (pulling the canonical openai/human-eval dataset from GitHub and executing pass@1 for real in a sandbox) and scored gpt-5.5 at 100.0% (40/40) -- but on only a 40-problem seeded subset (not the full 164; the source itself flags 'wide error bars' on a subset this small) and with no reasoning-effort parameter set anywhere in the repo's model config (cannot confirm this ran at 'xhigh' vs. default effort, the specific config this corpus entry represents) -- REJECTED as too noisy and effort-ambiguous to stand in for a canonical, xhigh-attributable HumanEval score.
  - KxSystems/q-evaluation-harness's gpt-5.5 model card reports 94.5%/88.4% on 'q-humaneval (164 problems)' at 'high' (not xhigh) reasoning effort -- but 'q-humaneval' is KX Systems' own q/kdb+ programming-language adaptation of HumanEval, a different benchmark from the standard Python HumanEval the corpus tracks (same category of rejection as the OTIS-Mock-AIME precedent from the prior round) -- REJECTED as non-comparable.
  - detandreas/DEA-LLMs's models_frontier.json carries an entry literally named 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' with mmlu_pro, math_500, aime, and aime_25 all explicitly null -- but its field schema (artificial_analysis_intelligence_index, gpqa=0.935, hle=0.443, etc.) shows this is simply a re-published mirror of Artificial Analysis's own API data, already exhausted as a source in round 1; treated as corroboration, not a new independent source.

(5) Third-party review/analysis sites: Checked both of Simon Willison's GPT-5.5 posts (the launch-day pelican-test post and the prompting-guide link post) -- neither contains any benchmark figures at all. interconnects.ai has no dedicated GPT-5.5 article (only older GPT-5/GPT-5.2 pieces). SemiAnalysis coverage found relates to GPT-5.2, not GPT-5.5. A Korean independent tech blog hosted on GitHub (ahastudio/til, openai/gpt-5-5.md) explicitly states in its own-authored analysis (translated): 'The most notable change in the GPT-5.5 announcement is the kind of benchmark. Knowledge/coding exams like MMLU and HumanEval have receded into the background' as the benchmark suite shifted to agentic evals (Terminal-Bench 2.0, GDPval, Expert-SWE, OSWorld-Verified, BrowseComp) -- an independent, freshly-found corroboration (not checked in round 2's non-English pass) of the exact same absence pattern. Separately, Whamp/substitutionbench's internal methodology doc (saturated-benchmarks-and-source-audit.md) explicitly names GPT-5.5 in its 'frontier_set' and documents, as general policy, that saturated legacy benchmarks (its examples include math_500) get silently dropped from current-frontier leaderboard rows industry-wide, requiring an explicit 'assumed_frontier_score' placeholder rather than a real observation when this happens -- useful meta-corroboration for why these four benchmarks are absent, but by the doc's own definition an assumed/placeholder value, not a citable real score, so nothing was taken from it.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval remain omitted_no_evidence after this third pass. Four of the five prescribed new source types (conference talks, investor/press, GitHub issues/repos, third-party analysis sites) were fully and conclusively checked with no citable score surfacing anywhere, plus multiple newly-found independent sources actively corroborating the absence (community model catalogs marking these fields null/not-published, a contamination-probe research repo, and an independent Korean blog). The fifth (Wayback Machine) was genuinely attempted via two different tools and six retries but could not be completed due to a persistent, environment-level rate-limit/network-reputation block on archive.org from this sandbox, not from a search that turned up nothing -- flagging this specifically in case a future pass has cleaner network access to actually diff the archived vs. live launch page. This is now the third independent round, across three disjoint sets of source types, to reach the same conclusion, consistent with the taxonomy's framing of these four as saturated/retired benchmarks the frontier labs and independent evaluators alike have stopped running for the GPT-5.5 generation.

2026-07-18 FOURTH-PASS targeted deep dive specifically on livecodebench, swe-bench, and mmmu -- the three benchmarks last touched directly only in the original 2026-07-16 completeness check (rounds 2 and 3 on 2026-07-17/18 focused exclusively on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval and explicitly left these three out of scope). Result: swe-bench RESOLVED with a real, effort-matched independent score added to results[]; livecodebench and mmmu remain omitted_no_evidence after exhausting all 6 prescribed research methods for each.

SWE-BENCH -- FOUND, ADDED. LM Council's benchmark hub (lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks), whose SWE-bench Verified table is sourced from Epoch AI, carries an explicit 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' row at 80.6% +/-1.8 -- exactly matching this catalog entry's effortLabel. See the new results[] entry for full sourcing detail and the three rejected alternative figures (OpenAI's self-reported SWE-bench-Pro 58.6%, an unsourced 88.7% circulating on marc0.dev/tokenmix.ai, and Vals.ai's non-effort-specific 82.60%).

LIVECODEBENCH -- still omitted_no_evidence after checking: (1) official launch materials -- benchlm.ai's exhaustive ~60-benchmark GPT-5.5 table and vellum.ai's comprehensive launch-page transcription both omit LiveCodeBench entirely; (2) independent trackers -- Artificial Analysis (GPT-5.5(xhigh) model page and dedicated LiveCodeBench spotlight leaderboard both re-confirmed to exclude every GPT-5.5 variant), Vals.ai's dedicated LiveCodeBench leaderboard (vals.ai/benchmarks/lcb, no GPT-5.5 entry at all -- top models are Claude Fable 5/Gemini 3.1 Pro/GPT-5.2 Codex), LM Council (no LiveCodeBench table exists on the page), and CodeSOTA.com's code-generation leaderboard (explicitly confirms 'GPT-5.5 does not appear' in its SWE-bench Verified/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/Aider Polyglot tables -- the page's only GPT-5.5 mention is an unrelated 'DeepSWE[xhigh] 70%+/-4' citation from a third-party Datacurve leaderboard, a different benchmark entirely); (3) the official LiveCodeBench leaderboard itself (livecodebench.github.io/leaderboard.html) was fetched directly -- no GPT-5.5 row present in retrievable content, consistent with web-search coverage of the same leaderboard showing only GPT-5.2 Codex/GPT-5 Mini as the newest OpenAI entries; (4) sibling-model comparison tables -- several independent Gemini-3.5-Flash-vs-GPT-5.5-vs-Opus-4.7 three-way comparison articles were checked and none cites a LiveCodeBench figure for GPT-5.5 (they compare on MMMU-Pro, SWE-bench Pro, and token throughput instead). One low-quality source (automatio.ai) lists 'LiveCodeBench: 78%', but this is the same previously-rejected, uncitationed content-mill page, and its adjacent 'SWE-Bench: 58.6%' entry directly conflates the SWE-bench-Pro figure with plain SWE-bench (a mislabeling caught fresh this round) -- further discrediting it as a source, not used.

MMMU -- still omitted_no_evidence. Model confirmed vision-capable (modalities includes 'vision', capabilities.vision=true) so this is a genuine reporting gap, NOT architectural inapplicability. Checked: (1) official launch materials report 'MMMU-Pro (no tools): 81.2%' and 'MMMU-Pro (with tools/w Python): 83.2%' (kingy.ai, benchlm.ai) with no base-MMMU figure anywhere; (2) Artificial Analysis tracks MMMU-Pro only on its GPT-5.5(xhigh) model page, and a direct fetch of AA's would-be dedicated base-MMMU evaluation page (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/mmmu) returned HTTP 404, confirming AA maintains no base-MMMU eval page for this benchmark generation; (3) Vals.ai's page titled 'MMMU' (vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu) is, on direct inspection, actually MMMU-Pro under a shortened URL/title (confirmed via its own dataset description: 'MMMU Pro encompasses over 1,000 high-quality tasks...') -- not a genuine base-MMMU source, and reports an un-disaggregated 88.27% with no effort breakdown; (4) LM Council's hub carries no MMMU or MMMU-Pro table at all; (5) sibling-model comparison tables -- every checked Gemini-3.5-Flash/Claude-Opus-4.7/GPT-5.5 comparison article uses MMMU-Pro as the shared vision benchmark, never base MMMU. One low-quality source (automatio.ai) lists both 'MMMU: 88.3%' and 'MMMU Pro: 62%' with no citations or effort labels -- the same previously-rejected content-mill page, and its MMMU-Pro figure (62%) directly contradicts the well-corroborated official 81-83% MMMU-Pro figures from kingy.ai/benchlm.ai for the same model, an internal-consistency red flag that further disqualifies it. Conclusion: base MMMU appears fully superseded by MMMU-Pro industry-wide for the GPT-5.5 generation (paralleling the swe-bench/swe-bench-pro and mmlu/mmlu-pro succession already documented in this entry's history) -- no citable base-MMMU score exists for GPT-5.5 (xhigh) or any sibling configuration after checking 5 of 6 prescribed method categories this round.
